18	end

22	  assert(testerr(msg, f, ...))
23	end
24	
25	
26	-- cannot close standard files
27	assert(not io.close(io.stdin) and
28	       not io.stdout:close() and
29	       not io.stderr:close())
30	
31	-- cannot call close method without an argument (new in 5.3.5)
32	checkerr("got no value", io.stdin.close)
33	
34	
35	assert(type(io.input()) == "userdata" and io.type(io.output()) == "file")
36	assert(type(io.stdin) == "userdata" and io.type(io.stderr) == "file")
37	assert(not io.type(8))
38	local a = {}; setmetatable(a, {})
39	assert(not io.type(a))
40	
41	assert(getmetatable(io.input()).__name == "FILE*")
42	
43	local a,b,c = io.open('xuxu_nao_existe')
44	assert(not a and type(b) == "string" and type(c) == "number")
45	
46	a,b,c = io.open('/a/b/c/d', 'w')
47	assert(not a and type(b) == "string" and type(c) == "number")
48	
49	local file = os.tmpname()
50	local f, msg = io.open(file, "w")
51	if not f then
52	  (Message or print)("'os.tmpname' file cannot be open; skipping file tests")
53	
54	else  --{  most tests here need tmpname
55	f:close()
56	
57	print('testing i/o')
58	
59	local otherfile = os.tmpname()
60	
61	checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "rw")
62	checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "rb+")
63	checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "r+bk")
64	checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "")
65	checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "+")
66	checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "b")
67	assert(io.open(file, "r+b")):close()
68	assert(io.open(file, "r+")):close()
69	assert(io.open(file, "rb")):close()
70	
71	assert(os.setlocale('C', 'all'))
72	
73	io.input(io.stdin); io.output(io.stdout);
74	
75	os.remove(file)
76	assert(not loadfile(file))
77	checkerr("", dofile, file)
78	assert(not io.open(file))
79	io.output(file)
80	assert(io.output() ~= io.stdout)
81	
82	if not _port then   -- invalid seek
83	  local status, msg, code = io.stdin:seek("set", 1000)
84	  assert(not status and type(msg) == "string" and type(code) == "number")
85	end
86	
87	assert(io.output():seek() == 0)
88	assert(io.write("alo alo"):seek() == string.len("alo alo"))
89	assert(io.output():seek("cur", -3) == string.len("alo alo")-3)
90	assert(io.write("joao"))
91	assert(io.output():seek("end") == string.len("alo joao"))
92	
93	assert(io.output():seek("set") == 0)
94	
95	assert(io.write('"alo"', "{a}\n", "second line\n", "third line \n"))
96	assert(io.write('Xfourth_line'))
97	io.output(io.stdout)
98	collectgarbage()  -- file should be closed by GC
99	assert(io.input() == io.stdin and rawequal(io.output(), io.stdout))
100	print('+')

file.content:
-- $Id: testes/files.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

local debug = require "debug"

local maxint = math.maxinteger

assert(type(os.getenv"PATH") == "string")

assert(io.input(io.stdin) == io.stdin)
assert(not pcall(io.input, "non-existent-file"))
assert(io.output(io.stdout) == io.stdout)


local function testerr (msg, f, ...)
  local stat, err = pcall(f, ...)
  return (not stat and string.find(err, msg, 1, true))
end


local function checkerr (msg, f, ...)
  assert(testerr(msg, f, ...))
end


-- cannot close standard files
assert(not io.close(io.stdin) and
       not io.stdout:close() and
       not io.stderr:close())

-- cannot call close method without an argument (new in 5.3.5)
checkerr("got no value", io.stdin.close)


assert(type(io.input()) == "userdata" and io.type(io.output()) == "file")
assert(type(io.stdin) == "userdata" and io.type(io.stderr) == "file")
assert(not io.type(8))
local a = {}; setmetatable(a, {})
assert(not io.type(a))

assert(getmetatable(io.input()).__name == "FILE*")

local a,b,c = io.open('xuxu_nao_existe')
assert(not a and type(b) == "string" and type(c) == "number")

a,b,c = io.open('/a/b/c/d', 'w')
assert(not a and type(b) == "string" and type(c) == "number")

local file = os.tmpname()
local f, msg = io.open(file, "w")
if not f then
  (Message or print)("'os.tmpname' file cannot be open; skipping file tests")

else  --{  most tests here need tmpname
f:close()

print('testing i/o')

local otherfile = os.tmpname()

checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "rw")
checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "rb+")
checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "r+bk")
checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "")
checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "+")
checkerr("invalid mode", io.open, file, "b")
assert(io.open(file, "r+b")):close()
assert(io.open(file, "r+")):close()
assert(io.open(file, "rb")):close()

assert(os.setlocale('C', 'all'))

io.input(io.stdin); io.output(io.stdout);

os.remove(file)
assert(not loadfile(file))
checkerr("", dofile, file)
assert(not io.open(file))
io.output(file)
assert(io.output() ~= io.stdout)

if not _port then   -- invalid seek
  local status, msg, code = io.stdin:seek("set", 1000)
  assert(not status and type(msg) == "string" and type(code) == "number")
end

assert(io.output():seek() == 0)
assert(io.write("alo alo"):seek() == string.len("alo alo"))
assert(io.output():seek("cur", -3) == string.len("alo alo")-3)
assert(io.write("joao"))
assert(io.output():seek("end") == string.len("alo joao"))

assert(io.output():seek("set") == 0)

assert(io.write('"alo"', "{a}\n", "second line\n", "third line \n"))
assert(io.write('Xfourth_line'))
io.output(io.stdout)
collectgarbage()  -- file should be closed by GC
assert(io.input() == io.stdin and rawequal(io.output(), io.stdout))
print('+')

1790	}
1791	
1792	/// Gets the n-th local name from a Lua closure (for non-active function query).
1793	/// C: `luaF_getlocalname(cl->p, n, 0)`.
1794	/// TODO(port): full implementation lives in crate::func
1795	fn get_local_name_from_closure(cl: &LuaClosureLua, n: i32, pc: i32) -> Option<&[u8]> {
1796	    get_local_name(&cl.proto, n, pc)
1797	}
1798	
1799	/// Retrieves the LuaProto for the Lua closure at `ci.func` from the stack.
1800	///
1801	/// C: `ci_func(ci)->p`  — the proto pointer of the Lua closure for this frame.
1802	/// macros.tsv: ci_func → ci.lua_closure() returning &GcRef<LuaClosure::Lua>
1803	///
1804	/// PORT NOTE: The C version returns a raw pointer and is a macro. Here we
1805	/// navigate through the LuaState stack. Returns a reference with the
1806	/// lifetime of the proto inside the GcRef (Rc), which must remain valid.
1807	///
1808	/// TODO(port): This returns a cloned Rc's inner reference; Phase B must verify
1809	/// lifetimes are correct once all types are wired.
1810	/// PORT NOTE: reshaped for borrowck — returns `GcRef<LuaProto>` (Rc clone) instead
1811	/// of `&'a LuaProto` to avoid returning a reference to a temporary `LuaValue`
1812	/// produced by `get_at`. Callers deref through `GcRef<T>: Deref<Target=T>`.
1813	fn ci_lua_proto(ci: &CallInfo, state: &LuaState) -> GcRef<LuaProto> {
1814	    match state.get_at(ci.func) {
1815	        LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)) => cl.proto.clone(),
1816	        _ => panic!("ci_lua_proto: call frame does not hold a Lua closure"),
1817	    }
1818	}
1819	
1820	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1821	// PORT STATUS
1822	//   source:        src/ldebug.c  (962 lines, 30 functions)
1823	//   target_crate:  lua-vm
1824	//   confidence:    medium
1825	//   todos:         44
1826	//   port_notes:    15
1827	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
1828	//   notes:         Logic faithful to C; cross-crate imports (luaF_*, luaT_*,
1829	//                  luaD_*, luaO_chunkid, opcode accessors) are stubbed with
1830	//                  TODO(port) markers. LuaState accessor methods (call_stack_mut,
1831	//                  get_ci, set_trap, saved_pc, hook_mask, etc.) are called as if
1832	//                  defined in state.rs — Phase B must implement them. The
1833	//                  pointer-identity comparisons in instack/getupvalname are
1834	//                  translated to StackIdx comparisons (a structural change).
1835	//                  `lua_gethook` returns a bool instead of a fn pointer because
1836	//                  Box<dyn FnMut> cannot be returned by value without restructuring.
1837	//                  rustc check: zero real syntax errors; all 67 diagnostics are
1838	//                  expected name-resolution errors (E0432/E0433/E0425/E0282).
1839	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1840

delta.text: Found it. C's `isLua(ci)` is `!(ci->callstatus & CIST_C)` —
checks the callstatus bit, not the union variant. Our Rust `is_lua()` checks the `u` variant which can be out of sync with `callstatus`. The
mismatched frame has `callstatus=CIST_C` (C frame) but `u=CallInfoFrame::Lua`
(default).

delta.text: Compile

is clean. Per instructions I won't re-run the program.

**Root cause:** `CallInfo::is_lua
()` at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:309` returned `matches!(self.u, CallInfoF
rame::Lua { .. })`, but in C-Lua the canonical truth is `!(ci->callstat
us & CIST_C)`. `prep_call_info` (`crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:743
`) only writes `callstatus`, not the `u` variant, so the `u` field was stale from whatever previously inhabited that C
allInfo slot. When a C call (e.g. `pcall`→`f_close`) reused a sl
ot last used by a Lua frame, `is_lua()` returned `true` even though the callstatus said `CIST_C`
, and `debug.rs:1816 ci_lua_proto` panicked because the slot held

a C closure.

**Fix:**
1. `CallInfo::is_lua()` now checks `(self.callstatus & CIST_C) == 0`

, matching C semantics.
2. `prep_call_info` now also resets `ci.u` to `c_default()` or `lua_default
()` based on the mask, so `saved_pc()`/`nextra_args()`

/etc. observe a consistent payload.

Temporary `eprintln!` remov
ed. `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Did not re-test per instructions; the
mega-loop will re-run.

content.text:
Compile is clean. Per instructions I won't re-run the program.

**Root cause:** `CallInfo::is_lua()` at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:309` returned `matches!(self.u, CallInfoFrame::Lua { .. })`, but in C-Lua the canonical truth is `!(ci->callstatus & CIST_C)`. `prep_call_info` (`crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:743`) only writes `callstatus`, not the `u` variant, so the `u` field was stale from whatever previously inhabited that CallInfo slot. When a C call (e.g. `pcall`→`f_close`) reused a slot last used by a Lua frame, `is_lua()` returned `true` even though the callstatus said `CIST_C`, and `debug.rs:1816 ci_lua_proto` panicked because the slot held a C closure.

**Fix:**
1. `CallInfo::is_lua()` now checks `(self.callstatus & CIST_C) == 0`, matching C semantics.
2. `prep_call_info` now also resets `ci.u` to `c_default()` or `lua_default()` based on the mask, so `saved_pc()`/`nextra_args()`/etc. observe a consistent payload.

Temporary `eprintln!` removed. `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. Did not re-test per instructions; the mega-loop will re-run.
